h1: Guatapé y El Peñol: vocabulario de inglés para el trabajo
question: ¿Qué necesito saber de inglés para "Guatapé y El Peñol"?
intro: Para "Guatapé y El Peñol" en el trabajo necesitas estas 16 palabras y frases de inglés - vocabulario real del corpus de tarjetas de HolaRuta · Inglés, listo para practicar.

sections.heading: Frases esenciales
bullets: "Buenos días a todos, bienvenidos al tour a Guatapé." - "Good morning, everyone, and welcome to the Guatapé tour." (good MOR-ning EV-ree-wun, and WEL-cum to de gwa-ta-PAY toor)

"Guatapé queda a unas dos horas de Medellín, así que salimos ya." - "Guatapé is about a two-hour drive from Medellín, so we're heading out now." (gwa-ta-PAY iz a-BOUT a TOO-owr drive from meh-deh-YEEN, so weer HED-ing out now)

"Por favor pónganse zapatos cómodos, hoy vamos a caminar bastante." - "Please wear comfortable shoes, because we'll be doing a lot of walking today." (pleez wair CUMF-ta-bul shooz, bee-KUZ weel bee DOO-ing a lot of WAW-king too-DAY)

"No olviden traer agua y protector solar, el sol pega fuerte." - "Don't forget to bring water and sunscreen; the sun is strong up here." (dont for-GET to bring WAW-ter and SUN-screen; de sun iz strong up heer)
"Nuestra primera parada es La Piedra del Peñol." - "Our first stop is La Piedra del Peñol, the great rock." (owr furst stop iz la pee-EH-dra del peh-NYOL, de grayt rock)

"Esta roca es un monolito gigante de unos doscientos metros de alto." - "This rock is a giant monolith about two hundred meters tall." (dis rock iz a JY-ant MON-oh-lith a-BOUT too HUN-dred MEE-ters tall)

"Después vamos al pueblo de Guatapé, famoso por sus zócalos de colores." - "Afterwards we'll head into the town of Guatapé, famous for its colorful zócalos." (AF-ter-wardz weel hed IN-too de town of gwa-ta-PAY, FAY-mus for its CUL-er-ful SO-ka-los)

sections.heading: Más vocabulario útil
bullets: "Se sube por una escalera de unos setecientos cuarenta escalones construida en la grieta." - "You climb it on a staircase of about seven hundred and forty steps built into the crack." (yoo klime it on a STAIR-case of a-BOUT SEV-en HUN-dred and FOR-tee steps bilt IN-too de crack)

"Tómense su tiempo en las escaleras, no hay prisa, suban a su propio ritmo." - "Take your time on the steps; there's no rush, just go at your own pace." (tayk yor time on de steps; dairz no rush, just go at yor own payss)
"Desde arriba tendrán una vista panorámica del embalse." - "From the top you'll get a panoramic view over the reservoir." (from de top yool get a pan-oh-RAM-ic vyoo OH-ver de REZ-er-vwar)

"El embalse es un laberinto de islas y agua, es impresionante." - "The reservoir is a maze of islands and water; it's absolutely stunning." (de REZ-er-vwar iz a mayz of EYE-landz and WAW-ter; its AB-so-loot-lee STUN-ning)
"Hay que pagar una entrada para subir la piedra." - "There's an admission fee to climb the rock." (dairz an ad-MISH-un fee to klime de rock)

"Los zócalos son paneles pintados en relieve en la parte baja de las casas." - "The zócalos are painted relief panels along the lower walls of the houses." (de SO-ka-los ar PAYN-ted ree-LEEF PAN-elz a-LONG de LOH-er wallz of de HOW-siz)
"Luego haremos un paseo en lancha por el embalse." - "Later we'll take a boat tour around the reservoir." (LAY-ter weel tayk a boat toor a-ROUND de REZ-er-vwar)

"El embalse inundó el viejo Peñol, y bajo el agua quedó el pueblo antiguo y una mansión de Pablo Escobar." - "The reservoir flooded the old town of El Peñol, and beneath the water lie its ruins along with a sunken Pablo Escobar mansion." (de REZ-er-vwar FLUD-ed de old town of el peh-NYOL, and bee-NEETH de WAW-ter lai its RÚ-inz a-LONG with a SUNK-en PAB-lo es-ko-BAR MAN-shun)

"Almorzamos en el pueblo y el bus regresa a las cuatro; por favor no se alejen del grupo." - "We'll have lunch in town and the bus leaves at four, so please don't wander off from the group." (weel hav lunch in town and de bus leevz at for, so pleez dont WON-der off from de groop)

question: ¿Qué necesito saber de inglés para "Nómadas y seguridad"?
intro: 16 palabras y frases de inglés para dominar "Nómadas y seguridad" con huéspedes y turistas - directo del corpus real de HolaRuta · Inglés, sin relleno de manual.

sections.heading: Frases esenciales
bullets: "¿Cuánto tiempo te vas a quedar en Medellín?" - "How long are you staying in Medellín?" (how LONG are you STAY-ing in meh-deh-YEEN)
"Bienvenido a Medellín. ¿Es tu primera vez aquí?" - "Welcome to Medellín! Is this your first time here?" (WEL-come to meh-deh-YEEN. iz dis your FIRST time heer)

"El Poblado y Provenza tienen los mejores cafés y la vida nocturna." - "El Poblado and Provenza have the best cafes and nightlife." (el poh-BLAH-doh and proh-VEN-sah hav de best kah-FAYZ and NITE-life)
"El clima es agradable todo el año, no necesitas abrigo." - "The weather is nice all year round, so you don't need a coat." (de WEH-der iz nice all yeer round, so you dont need a KOHT)
"Lleva una sombrilla, por la tarde suele llover." - "Bring an umbrella, it usually rains in the afternoon." (bring an um-BREL-la, it YOO-zhoo-al-lee raynz in de af-ter-NOON)
"¿Necesitas una tarjeta SIM con datos?" - "Do you need a SIM card with data?" (do you need a SIM card wid DAY-ta)

sections.heading: Más vocabulario útil
bullets: "Si buscas algo más tranquilo, Laureles es más caminable." - "If you want somewhere calmer, Laureles is more walkable." (if you want SUM-wair KAH-mer, lau-REH-less iz mor WAWK-a-bul)

"Envigado es más residencial y auténtico, y también me gusta." - "Envigado is more residential and authentic, and I like it too." (en-vee-GAH-doh iz mor reh-zih-DEN-shul and aw-ZEN-tik, and I like it TOO)
"Hay muchos espacios de coworking con internet rápido." - "There are lots of coworking spaces with fast internet." (dair are lots of koh-WORK-ing SPAY-ces wid fast IN-ter-net)
"Te puedo recomendar un café tranquilo para trabajar." - "I can recommend a quiet cafe to work from." (I can reh-koh-MEND a KWY-et kah-FAY to WORK from)

"Para estadías largas, es más barato un apartamento amoblado por mes." - "For long stays, a furnished apartment by the month is cheaper." (for long stayz, a FUR-nisht a-PART-ment by de MUNZ iz CHEE-per)
"Aquí decimos "no dar papaya": no muestres cosas de valor." - "Here we say "don't give papaya": don't flaunt your valuables." (heer we say dont giv pa-PA-ya: dont FLAWNT your VAL-yoo-a-bulz)

"Guarda el celular en el bolsillo, no camines con él en la mano." - "Keep your phone in your pocket, don't walk around with it in your hand." (keep your fone in your POK-et, dont wawk a-ROUND wid it in your hand)
"Cuida tu bolso y evita ciertos barrios muy tarde en la noche." - "Watch your bag and avoid certain neighborhoods late at night." (wawch your bag and a-VOID SER-tin NAY-bor-hoodz layt at NITE)

"De noche, mejor pide un Uber, DiDi o Cabify que caminar." - "At night, it's better to order an Uber, DiDi or Cabify than to walk." (at nite, its BEH-ter to OR-der an OO-ber, DEE-dee or KAB-ih-fy dan to wawk)
"Saca plata en un cajero dentro de un centro comercial o banco." - "Take cash out at an ATM inside a mall or a bank." (tayk cash out at an ay-tee-EM in-SIDE a mawl or a bank)

h1: Feria y eventos: vocabulario de inglés para el trabajo
question: ¿Qué necesito saber de inglés para "Feria y eventos"?
intro: Estas 16 palabras y frases de inglés para "Feria y eventos" te ayudan a atender a huéspedes y turistas en tu trabajo - vocabulario real, extraído del corpus de tarjetas de HolaRuta · Inglés.

sections.heading: Frases esenciales
bullets: "¿Cuál es el festival más importante de Medellín?" - "Our biggest festival is the Feria de las Flores, the Flower Festival — the whole city fills with flowers, parades, and music." (OW-er BIG-est FES-ti-val iz da FEH-ree-ah de las FLOH-res, da FLOW-er FES-ti-val.)

"¿Qué es el Desfile de Silleteros?" - "The Desfile de Silleteros is the main parade, where flower farmers carry huge flower arrangements called silletas on their backs." (Da pah-RAYD... FLOW-er FAR-merz KAH-ree HYOOJ FLOW-er ah-RAYNJ-ments on dair baks.)

"¿Quiénes son los silleteros?" - "The silleteros are flower farmers from Santa Elena, a village up in the mountains, and they've grown flowers for generations." (Da FLOW-er FAR-merz ar from SAN-tah eh-LAY-nah, a VIH-lij up in da MOWN-tinz.)

"¿Cuánto cuesta ver el desfile?" - "It's completely free to watch. You just find a spot along the route, and it's very family-friendly." (Its kom-PLEET-lee FREE to woch. Yoo just fynd a SPOT ah-LONG da ROOT.)

"¿En qué mes es la Feria de las Flores?" - "The Flower Festival is in August, usually starting in the first week and running for about ten days." (Da FLOW-er FES-ti-val iz in AW-gust, YOO-zhoo-al-lee STAR-ting in da furst week.)

"¿Los Alumbrados Navideños son famosos?" - "Yes, the Christmas lights along the Medellín River are famous, one of the best light displays in the world." (Yes, da KRIS-mus lyts ah-LONG da meh-deh-YEEN RIH-ver ar FAY-mus.)

sections.heading: Más vocabulario útil
bullets: "¿Qué otros eventos tiene la Feria de las Flores?" - "Besides the parade, there's a classic car parade, concerts, an orchid show, and in some years a horseback parade called the cabalgata." (bi-SÁIDZ da pa-RÉID, dairz a KLA-sik kar pa-RÉID, KON-serts, an OR-kid shóu, and SÁM-taimz a HORS-bak pa-RÉID.)

"¿Qué son las trovas?" - "Trovas are improvised songs in the paisa tradition. Two singers trade witty, rhyming verses back and forth, and it's hilarious." (TROH-vahz ar IM-pro-vyzd songz in da PYE-sah tra-DIH-shun. Too SING-erz trayd WIH-tee RY-ming VUR-sez.)

"¿Por qué debería reservar el hotel con anticipación?" - "During the festival the whole city fills up, so I'd really recommend booking your hotel well ahead of time." (DUR-ing da FES-ti-val da hohl SIH-tee filz up, so eyd REE-lee reh-koh-MEND BUUK-ing yor hoh-TEL.)

"¿A qué hora debo llegar para ver el desfile?" - "Arrive early, at least a couple of hours before, to get a good spot. The crowds are huge but friendly." (Ah-RYV UR-lee, at leest a KUH-pul of OWRZ bee-FOR, to get a guud SPOT. Da krowdz ar HYOOJ.)

"¿Qué es el Día de las Velitas?" - "The Day of the Little Candles is on December 7th. People light candles and lanterns everywhere, and it kicks off the Christmas season." (Da DAY of da LIH-tul KAN-dulz iz on dee-SEM-ber SEV-enz. PEE-pul lyt KAN-dulz EV-ree-wair.)

"¿Qué es Colombiamoda?" - "Colombiamoda is Colombia's biggest fashion week, held here in Medellín in July, with runway shows and designers from all over." (koh-LOM-bee-ah-MOH-dah iz koh-LOM-bee-ahz BIG-est FA-shun week, held heer in meh-deh-YEEN in joo-LY.)

"¿Cómo es el clima durante la Feria?" - "The weather is lovely, around 22 degrees, spring-like all year. Bring a light jacket for the evenings and an umbrella just in case." (Da WEH-der iz LUV-lee, ah-ROWND TWEN-tee-too dih-GREEZ, SPRING-lyk all yeer. Bring a lyt JA-ket.)

"¿Dónde puedo ver la exhibición de orquídeas?" - "The orchid exhibition is at the Botanical Garden. Colombia has thousands of orchid species, so it's really worth a visit." (Da OR-kid ek-si-BIH-shun iz at da boh-TA-ni-kal GAR-den. koh-LOM-bee-ah haz ZOW-zundz of OR-kid SPEE-sheez.)

"¿Vale la pena subir a Santa Elena a ver los cultivos de flores?" - "Absolutely. You can go up to Santa Elena, visit a silletero farm, and see how they grow the flowers and build the silletas." (AB-so-loot-lee. Yoo kan goh up to SAN-tah eh-LAY-nah, VIH-zit a farm, and see how day groh da FLOW-erz.)

"¿Dónde termina el Desfile de Silleteros?" - "The parade winds through the city and finishes near the stadium area, where the silleteros are judged and celebrated." (Da pah-RAYD wyndz throo da SIH-tee and FIH-nih-shez neer da STAY-dee-um AIR-ee-ah.)
